sourceMovie.title: Underworld
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Underworld works as a fantasy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The fantasy and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Blade II
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Underworld clicked for you, Blade II (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and action, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Underworld?
faq.answer: Try Blade II, Dracula Untold, Sinners—each shares DNA with Underworld while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Underworld?
faq.answer: Underworld (2003) is best described as Fantasy, Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Underworld part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Underworld Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
